question.text: The distinction between speculative and hedging transactions in commodity derivatives affects:

answer.text: Tax treatment — hedging income is business income while speculative income has set-off restrictions
Exchange fees — hedgers pay lower fees
Margin requirements — speculators need no margin

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Tax treatment is the key difference: hedging income = normal business income (set off against other business losses). Speculative income = speculative business income (speculative losses only against speculative profits). **The Trap:** Exchange fees don't differ by trader type. All participants need margins. The distinction is very meaningful. **Cross-Exam Context:** Tax classification of derivative income is important across exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** To qualify for hedging treatment, maintain: (1) documentation of physical exposure, (2) matching delivery dates, (3) proportionate position sizes, (4) clear business rationale.
